title: Chassis exemption registry
description: Time-bounded waiver registry for Chassis findings. Each entry grants a narrowly-scoped, owner-accountable, expiring exemption for a specific rule (or finding id). Audit suppresses a finding only when an entry is **active**, scope-matched, and not expired. Expired or invalid entries become audit findings of their own. Wildcard / global scopes require an explicit `allow_global: true` and fail strict mode unless the registry-level `allow_global: true` is set. Schema bumped to 1.1 for lifecycle fields (`status`, `severity_override`, `linked_issue`, `created_at`/`expires_at`, `path`, `rule_id`, `finding_id`).

version.description: Registry schema major. 1 = legacy field names (rule, scope, created, expires). 2 = lifecycle fields (rule_id, path/scope, created_at, expires_at, status). Both are accepted; v2 is recommended for new entries.

allow_global.description: When true, individual entries may use scope wildcards (`*`, `**`, repository-root '/'). Strict profile still rejects global entries unless this flag and the per-entry `allow_global: true` are both set.

id.description: Format EX-YYYY-NNNN. Must be unique within the registry.

rule_id.description: Rule identifier this exemption suppresses. Either `rule_id` or `finding_id` must be set.

finding_id.description: Specific finding instance id (preferred when targeting one finding). Either `rule_id` or `finding_id` must be set.

scope.description: Repo-relative file path or glob (legacy alias for `path`).

path.description: Repo-relative file path or glob (preferred). Either `path` or `scope` must be set.

reason.description: Why this waiver is needed. Minimum 40 chars prevents drive-by 'todo later' suppressions.

owner.description: Email or handle accountable for resolving the waiver.

linked_issue.description: Optional issue tracker reference (URL or 'PROJ-123'). Strongly recommended.

adr.description: Optional ADR id that defines the rule being exempted.

created_at.description: ISO date the exemption was opened. Either `created_at` or `created` must be set.

expires_at.description: ISO date after which the exemption no longer suppresses findings. Either `expires_at` or `expires` must be set. Maximum: created+90 days (enforced by `chassis exemptions add`).

severity_override.description: Optional. When set, the suppressed finding is downgraded to this severity instead of dropped, so it remains visible in reports without failing.

status.description: Lifecycle state. `active` participates in suppression; `expired` is informational only and surfaces as an `expired_exemptions` audit entry; `revoked` is retained as historical evidence and never suppresses.

allow_global.description: Per-entry opt-in for wildcard / global scopes. Required when path/scope contains `*`, `**`, or matches the repository root. Strict profile additionally requires the registry-level `allow_global: true`.

entries.reason: Vendored upstream parser uses unwrap() on tokenizer state; rewrite tracked in CHASSIS-142 with 60-day target.
